description:
You are the lead recon engineer on a penetration-testing gig.  
    A teammate has saved a dotenv file at

        /home/user/pentest/.targets.env

    with the three variables your tools need:

        TARGET_A=192.168.1.10
        TARGET_B=10.0.0.5
        PORTS=22,80,443

    The framework you are using cannot read “dotenv” syntax directly.  
    It instead expects a plain–text plan file called

        /home/user/pentest/scan_plan.txt

    whose contents must follow *exactly* this format:

        TARGET_A=192.168.1.10
        TARGET_B=10.0.0.5
        PORTS=22,80,443

    Formatting rules that **will be automatically tested**:

    1. The variables must appear in precisely the order shown above: TARGET_A, TARGET_B, then PORTS.
    2. Each line must end with a single newline (Unix LF, 0x0A) and there must be **no** blank line at the end of the file.
    3. No extra spaces, tabs or comments are allowed anywhere in the file.

    Your task:

    • Load the variables from /home/user/pentest/.targets.env into the current shell environment (do not paste the values manually; actually read them from the file).  
    • Create /home/user/pentest/scan_plan.txt that satisfies the format rules above.  
    • Do everything using a **single compound shell command** executed from the terminal (for example by chaining operators like “;” or “&&”).  
    • After the file is created, print its contents to STDOUT so the automated grader can capture and verify them.

    Hint: one typical workflow is to “source” the dotenv file and then use printf or echo redirection, but any correct one-liner that fulfils the requirements is acceptable.
truth:
<!-- Pre-existing directory and file -->
    Directory: /home/user/pentest
    File: /home/user/pentest/.targets.env
    Contents (including trailing newlines):
        TARGET_A=192.168.1.10\n
        TARGET_B=10.0.0.5\n
        PORTS=22,80,443\n

    <!-- Expected artefact generated by the agent -->
    File to be created: /home/user/pentest/scan_plan.txt
    Required contents (exact, including trailing newlines):
        TARGET_A=192.168.1.10\n
        TARGET_B=10.0.0.5\n
        PORTS=22,80,443\n
